questions.question: Explain the fundamental difference between an Externally Owned Account (EOA) and a Contract Account in Ethereum. What are the key characteristics of each?
keyPoints: An EOA is controlled by a private key and has no associated code.
A Contract Account is controlled by its own smart contract code and has no private key.
Only an EOA can initiate a transaction; a Contract Account can only execute code in response to a received transaction.

questions.question: Describe the process of how a transaction is sent from one account to another on a blockchain like Ethereum, from initiation to finality.
keyPoints: An EOA holder signs a transaction with their private key, specifying the recipient and value.
The signed transaction is broadcast to the network and picked up by nodes.
Miners/Validators include the transaction in a new block and execute it, updating the global state.
The transaction is considered final after a sufficient number of subsequent blocks have been added to the chain.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a nonce in an Externally Owned Account?
keyPoints: It is a counter that tracks the number of transactions sent from the account.
Its primary purpose is to prevent replay attacks, ensuring each transaction can only be executed once.
